matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: RET MEN2 activating model (RET endocrine tumor activation model)
RET fusion model (RET oncogenic kinase-fusion model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a RET RET receptor kinase biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology and MEN2 and medullary thyroid carcinoma review context, Hirschsprung disease developmental context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed RET MEN2 activating model dosage suggests this sample carries the RET prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. RET MEN2 activating model is curated as a RET marker for MEN2 and medullary thyroid carcinoma review context, Hirschsprung disease developmental context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched RET MEN2 activating model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the RET prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
